notes: Toxicity signs: nausea, yellow vision, bradycardia, AV block. Fatal ventricular arrhythmias within 2-6 hours at 5mg IV.
Multi-organ failure within 24-72 hours. Profuse diarrhea, bone marrow suppression, cardiac arrest.
Irreversible bone marrow suppression, mucositis, renal failure within 48 hours. Leucovorin rescue critical.
Monitor INR closely. Bleeding risk highest 3-5 days post-overdose. Vitamin K reversal.
Toxicity >1.5mmol/L: tremor, ataxia. >3.0mmol/L: seizures, coma, renal failure within 24 hours.
Zero-order kinetics. Toxicity: nystagmus, ataxia, hypotension. Cardiac arrest at high IV rates.
Toxicity >20mcg/ml: seizures, arrhythmias. Fatal ventricular tachycardia within 4 hours.
Myocarditis risk. Seizures, coma, respiratory depression at overdose. Fatal agranulocytosis risk.
Hypotension, bradycardia with rapid IV. Pulmonary fibrosis with chronic use. QT prolongation.
Respiratory arrest within 5-10 minutes. Pinpoint pupils, coma. Naloxone reversal.
Prolonged hypotension possible with massive overdose.
Lactic acidosis risk in renal failure. GI symptoms common.
GI bleeding, renal failure at high doses. Seizures possible.
Serotonin syndrome risk with MAOIs. QT prolongation at extreme doses.
Rash, diarrhea common. Anaphylaxis rare but serious.
Myopathy, rhabdomyolysis risk. Monitor CK levels.
Tachycardia, tremor at high doses. Hypokalemia possible.
Seizures, serotonin syndrome. Respiratory depression within 1-2 hours.
Respiratory failure within 20-30 minutes. Miosis, coma. Naloxone required.
Severe hypoglycemia → cerebral edema, cardiac arrest within 1-2 hours.
Bradycardia, heart block, hypotension. Fatal asystole within 30 minutes IV.
Coma, respiratory depression, QT prolongation. Fatal arrhythmias possible.
Metabolic acidosis, tinnitus, cerebral edema. Fatal respiratory failure within 12 hours.
Respiratory depression, coma within 1 hour. Flumazenil reversal.
Seizures, cardiac arrhythmias, coma. Fatal within 6-12 hours.
Thyroid storm with massive chronic overdose.
Respiratory depression, coma. Fatal within 2 hours without support.
Fatal cardiac arrhythmias within 2-4 hours at high doses. QT prolongation.
Myopathy risk higher than other statins.
Serotonin syndrome, seizures, hypertension. Fatal cardiac arrhythmias.
Sedation, respiratory depression, NMS. Fatal within 4 hours.
QT prolongation, torsades de pointes. Fatal ventricular fibrillation.
Anticholinergic toxicity, seizures, fatal cardiac arrhythmias within 2 hours.
Bradycardia, hypotension, bronchospasm. Fatal cardiac arrest within 1 hour.
Respiratory arrest within 30 minutes. Naloxone essential.
Respiratory depression, fatal within 1 hour. Naloxone reversal.
Respiratory failure, especially in CYP2D6 ultra-rapid metabolizers.
Respiratory depression ceiling effect. Fatal with benzodiazepines.
Coma, respiratory arrest within 2 hours. Flumazenil reversal.
Complex sleep behaviors. Respiratory depression with CNS depressants.
Rapid respiratory arrest within 5 minutes IV. Flumazenil.
Hypertension, emergence delirium. Respiratory depression rare.
Rapid IV push → fatal hypotension/respiratory arrest within 1 minute. Propofol infusion syndrome.
Fatal hyperkalemia in susceptible patients. Malignant hyperthermia.
Asystole for 5-10 seconds normal. Fatal in asthma.
Seizures, coma, cardiac arrest within 30 minutes.
Fatal cardiac arrhythmias within 5-10 minutes. Lipid emulsion rescue.
Fatal ventricular fibrillation within 1 minute IV.
Fatal hypertension, tissue necrosis with extravasation.
Respiratory paralysis, complete heart block within 30 minutes.
Fatal ventricular fibrillation within 5 minutes rapid IV.
Serotonin syndrome with SSRIs. Hemolysis in G6PD deficiency.
Use with atropine for OP poisoning.
Iron chelator. ARDS risk with rapid infusion.
